description: Inject the Prisma client into every Fastify app via a server-core plugin. Today 36 route files read `(fastify as unknown as { prisma }).prisma`, but nothing ever decorates `fastify.prisma`, so it is undefined and the first DB call throws at runtime. Add a first-class prisma plugin, register it in createApp(), and replace the unsafe casts with a typed accessor.
steps: Create packages/server-core/src/plugins/prisma.ts: a fastify-plugin that imports the singleton `prisma` from @quant/database, calls `fastify.decorate('prisma', prisma)`, connects on ready (`await prisma.$connect()`), and disconnects on `fastify.addHook('onClose', () => prisma.$disconnect())`.

Add a TypeScript module augmentation so `FastifyInstance` has a typed `prisma: PrismaClient` (declare module 'fastify'). Put it in packages/server-core/src/types.ts (or the plugin file) and export it.

Register the prisma plugin inside createApp() in packages/server-core/src/app.ts (before route registration), gated by an option (e.g. createApp({ withPrisma: true })) defaulting to true so service-only apps can opt out.

Replace every `const prisma = (fastify as unknown as { prisma: PrismaClient }).prisma` cast in app route files with `const { prisma } = fastify` (now typed). Sweep all apps/*/backend/routes/*.ts (start: apps/quantmail/backend/routes/emails.ts:48).
Add @quant/database as a dependency of @quant/server-core if not already present; ensure prisma generate runs before typecheck (database package turbo.json already declares prisma inputs/outputs).
Add a backend unit/integration test (vitest) that builds the app via createApp() and asserts `app.prisma` is defined.
Run pnpm --filter @quant/server-core typecheck test, then pnpm typecheck && pnpm build.
acceptance_criteria: packages/server-core/src/plugins/prisma.ts exists and is registered in createApp()
FastifyInstance.prisma is typed (no `as unknown as` casts remain in app route files)
A test proves app.prisma is defined after createApp()
pnpm typecheck, pnpm test, pnpm build all green
